notes: ChemNet flagship-promotion candidate review. 4 of 5 hard gates FAIL on the held-out 2023-2024 cohort (n=9,836 game-sides across 5,010 unique games, season-disjoint from 2015-2022 train range). Gate 1 (Pearson r): 0.089 vs 0.30 needed -- model is essentially predicting the global mean. Gate 2 (synergy edge): GNN is -0.14% WORSE than the non-graph baseline, so the graph adjacency is contributing nothing. Gate 3 (synergy predicts residual): rho=0.026 with CI [0.0066, 0.0457] -- statistically separable from zero but well below the 0.10 useful-edge threshold. Gate 4 (calibration): RMSE 4.59 wOBA-points, above the 4.0 ceiling that a global-mean predictor would clear. Gate 5 (leakage): 0 game_pk overlap, PASS by construction. Gate 6 (informational decile lift): top-decile synergy game-sides outperform bottom by +0.25 wOBA-points, monotonically noisy. Verdict: NOT FLAGSHIP -- the dashboard's lineup-synergy / protection-effects framing is not supported by the held-out evidence; the shipped chemnet_v1.pt does not predict its own training target out-of-sample. Recommend retraining with larger graph cohort, opposing-pitcher representation, or reformulating the synergy claim as a residual-prediction task before any flagship promotion. Model checkpoint, src/analytics/chemnet.py, src/dashboard/views/chemnet_view.py NOT modified per task constraints.
